name: Sazerac

spec.method_detail: Chill a rocks glass. Rinse with absinthe and discard (or keep if preferred). Stir rye, syrup, and bitters with ice until well-chilled. Strain into the rinsed, chilled glass (no ice). Express lemon peel over top, DO NOT add to glass.

garnish: Lemon peel (expressed over, then discarded or placed on rim)
history: Possibly the oldest American cocktail — originated in New Orleans around 1850 at the Sazerac Coffee House. Antoine Amedie Peychaud, a Creole apothecary, served his homemade bitters in a cognac cocktail. The original used Sazerac de Forge et Fils cognac; after a phylloxera epidemic devastated French vineyards in the 1870s, rye whiskey became the standard. Named the official cocktail of New Orleans by the Louisiana legislature in 2008.
